---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---

```{python}
# eda_visualization_unrate_target.py

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from pathlib import Path

# =========================
# 0) Config
# =========================
BASE_PATH = Path("/Users/yuanyuan/Downloads/final_project")
DATA_PATH = BASE_PATH / "data/macro_merged_clean.csv"

FIG_PATH = BASE_PATH / "figures"
FIG_PATH.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

sns.set_style("whitegrid")

# =========================
# 1) Load data
# =========================
df = pd.read_csv(DATA_PATH, parse_dates=["date"])
df = df.set_index("date").sort_index()

print("Dataset shape:", df.shape)
print("Columns:", df.columns.tolist())
print("\n--- Descriptive Statistics ---")
print(df.describe().T)
print("\n--- Data Info ---")
print(df.info())

# Ensure usrec is int (0/1) for plotting/hue
if df["usrec"].dtype != "int64" and df["usrec"].dtype != "int32":
    df["usrec"] = df["usrec"].astype(int)

# =========================
# 2) Define Target and Predictors (NEW SETUP)
# =========================
# NEW: target is unemployment rate
TARGET = "unrate"

# NEW: recession indicator is now a predictor variable
PREDICTORS = ["cpi", "gs10", "indpro", "tb3ms", "usrec"]

# sanity check
missing_cols = [c for c in [TARGET] + PREDICTORS if c not in df.columns]
if missing_cols:
    raise ValueError(f"Missing columns in dataset: {missing_cols}")

# =========================
# 3) Time-series trend plots (all variables)
# =========================
for col in df.columns:
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))
    sns.lineplot(x=df.index, y=df[col])
    plt.title(f"Trend over time — {col}")
    plt.xlabel("Date")
    plt.ylabel(col)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(FIG_PATH / f"trend_{col}.png", dpi=300)
    plt.close()

# A nicer plot for TARGET with recession shading
plt.figure(figsize=(11, 4))
sns.lineplot(x=df.index, y=df[TARGET])
plt.title("Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over time with recession periods")
plt.xlabel("Date")
plt.ylabel("UNRATE")

# shade recession months (usrec==1)
recession = df["usrec"] == 1
# Shade contiguous recession blocks
in_block = False
start = None
for t, is_rec in recession.items():
    if is_rec and not in_block:
        in_block = True
        start = t
    if not is_rec and in_block:
        end = t
        plt.axvspan(start, end, alpha=0.2)
        in_block = False
# if recession continues to end
if in_block:
    plt.axvspan(start, df.index[-1], alpha=0.2)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(FIG_PATH / "unrate_with_recession_shading.png", dpi=300)
plt.close()

# =========================
# 4) Correlation heatmap for X (predictors only)
# =========================
X = df[PREDICTORS]
corr_X = X.corr()

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
sns.heatmap(corr_X, annot=True, fmt=".2f", cmap="coolwarm", linewidths=0.5)
plt.title("Correlation Heatmap — Predictors (X): CPI, GS10, INDPRO, TB3MS, USREC")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(FIG_PATH / "correlation_heatmap_predictors_X.png", dpi=300)
plt.close()

# (Optional) Full correlation heatmap including target (sometimes useful)
corr_full = df[[TARGET] + PREDICTORS].corr()
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
sns.heatmap(corr_full, annot=True, fmt=".2f", cmap="coolwarm", linewidths=0.5)
plt.title("Correlation Heatmap — Target + Predictors (UNRATE included)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(FIG_PATH / "correlation_heatmap_full_unrate_plus_X.png", dpi=300)
plt.close()

# =========================
# 5) Correlation with TARGET (UNRATE)
# =========================
corr_with_target = df[[TARGET] + PREDICTORS].corr()[TARGET].sort_values(ascending=False)

print("\nCorrelation with Target (UNRATE):")
print(corr_with_target)

# For bar chart, drop the target itself (=1.0) to make it readable
corr_bar = corr_with_target.drop(index=TARGET)

plt.figure(figsize=(7, 4))
plt.barh(corr_bar.index, corr_bar.values)
plt.title("Correlation with Target (UNRATE)")
plt.xlabel("Correlation")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(FIG_PATH / "correlation_with_unrate.png", dpi=300)
plt.close()

# =========================
# 6) Pairplot (use USREC as hue to show regime differences)
# =========================
# Keep it small and relevant: target + predictors
pair_cols = [TARGET] + PREDICTORS

# pairplot can be slow; reduce points if needed (optional)
# df_plot = df[pair_cols].sample(2000, random_state=42) if len(df) > 2000 else df[pair_cols]
df_plot = df[pair_cols]

sns.pairplot(
    df_plot,
    hue="usrec",
    diag_kind="kde",
    corner=True,
    plot_kws={"alpha": 0.4, "s": 12},
)
plt.suptitle("Pairwise Relationships (Hue = USREC: recession vs expansion)", y=1.02)
plt.savefig(FIG_PATH / "pairplot_unrate_predictors_hue_usrec.png", dpi=300)
plt.close()

# =========================
# 7) Distributions (all variables)
# =========================
for col in df.columns:
    plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
    sns.histplot(df[col], bins=30, kde=True)
    plt.title(f"Distribution of {col}")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(FIG_PATH / f"hist_{col}.png", dpi=300)
    plt.close()

# Extra: UNRATE distribution split by recession
plt.figure(figsize=(7, 4))
sns.kdeplot(data=df, x="unrate", hue="usrec", common_norm=False)
plt.title("UNRATE distribution by recession regime (USREC)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(FIG_PATH / "unrate_distribution_by_usrec.png", dpi=300)
plt.close()

print(f"\nAll EDA figures saved in: {FIG_PATH}")
print("\nNEW modeling setup reminder:")
print("Target (Y) =", TARGET)
print("Predictors (X) =", PREDICTORS)


```
